journal_entry.description: Borrowed $200,000 from bank at 6% annual interest, 3-year term
journal_entry.rationale: Cash received from bank loan increases cash asset. The loan principal creates a long-term liability. The 6% interest rate and semi-annual payment terms will affect future interest accrual and payment entries but do not impact the initial loan receipt. Since the term is 3 years, this is classified as a long-term liability.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Bank loan received in cash — cash asset increases, long-term loan liability increases. Interest terms affect future entries but not the initial borrowing entry.
